title: The Canterbury Tales. The Friar's Tale. by Geoffrey Chaucer — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
THE PROLOGUE.

This worthy limitour, this noble Frere,
He made always a manner louring cheer
Upon the Sompnour; but for honesty
No villain word as yet to him spake he:
But at the last he said unto the Wife:
"Dame," quoth he, "God give you right good life,
meaning: "The Canterbury Tales. The Friar's Tale." by Geoffrey Chaucer is a sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 416 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "THE PROLOGUE. ...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Geoffrey Chaucer's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
